frame_analyses.description: Trainee positioning instruments, preparing to enter trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good setup position with instruments ready

frame_analyses.description: Hand holding needle driver approaching trainer entry point
First instrument visible in trainer, beginning needle loading phase
Both instruments working on Penrose drain, placing first suture through marked location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument coordination, precise needle placement at first mark

frame_analyses.description: Continuing suture placement through drain, working on second mark
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth progression to second suture point

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw formation, creating initial loops for surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper double throw technique for surgeon's knot initiation

frame_analyses.description: Completing second throw, switching hands as required
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand switching between throws, good knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Executing third and final throw with proper hand alternation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent hand switching technique, secure knot completion

frame_analyses.description: Positioning scissors to cut suture ends close to knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper cutting technique with appropriate tail length

frame_analyses.description: Task completed with both suture ends cut
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean completion with secure knot and properly trimmed suture

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw clearly executed
Good single throw with proper hand switching from first throw
Final single throw with correct hand alternation, knot appears tight and secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit properly closed with no visible gaps after knot tightening

technique_summary: Excellent technical performance with proper surgeon's knot formation, correct hand switching between throws, and good suture placement accuracy. The trainee demonstrated smooth instrument control and efficient workflow.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle placement at the marked locations to reduce deviation penalties
Consider slightly faster instrument movements during suture placement phase
strengths: Perfect knot sequence with proper surgeon's knot and hand switching
Smooth instrument coordination throughout the procedure
Efficient timing well below the 112-second average
Clean suture cutting with appropriate tail length
